title: 10 possible side effects of Inula helenium (Elecampane) if used improperly
intro: Inula helenium, also known as elecampane, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating respiratory conditions and digestive issues. However, if used improperly, it can have several potential health side effects. Firstly, it can cause stomach irritation, nausea, and vomiting, especially if taken in large amounts. Secondly, it can lower blood sugar levels, which can be dangerous for people with diabetes or hypoglycemia. Thirdly, it can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and drugs metabolized by the liver, leading to adverse effects. Additionally, pregnant women should avoid using Inula helenium as it may stimulate uterine contractions and result in miscarriage. Lastly, overuse of this plant can lead to poisoning, characterized by symptoms such as dizziness, tremors, and seizures. It is crucial to consult with a healthcare provider before using Inula helenium or any other medicinal herb.
